title: Cabinet clears 2.5 lakh houses, ₹587 crore for PRLSI
content: State to join RDSS and ₹300 crore sanctioned as first instalment for preparations for Godavari Pushkarams, mid-day meals in junior colleges

title: Twisha Sharma’s husband sent to 7-day police remand; AIIMS Delhi forms panel for second autopsy
content: Police told The Hindu that during the remand period, the SIT probing the case will recreate the scene of crime along with seizing substantial evidence from him and interrogating him on various aspects

title: MHA warns of phishing campaign targeting users of lost or stolen iPhones
content: Hackers impersonate Apple Support and exploit victims’ urgency to locate or secure their missing devices through fraudulent SMS messages containing phishing links

title: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai booked over ‘objectionable’ comment on PM Modi
content: Mr. Rai hit back calling the FIR an intimidation tactic and accusing the BJP government of misusing the State apparatus and police

title: Uppal flyover works push traffic into residential lanes in eastern Hyderabad, double commute times for residents
content: Diversions through colony roads hit local businesses, parking and daily commute at Uppal X roads and surrounding areas

title: Nursing student dies during surgery at Tiruchi government hospital; colleagues stage protest
content: A high-level inquiry committee, comprising doctors of the Madras Medical College and Rajiv Gandhi Government General Hospital (RGGGH), has been constituted to inquire into the incident

title: A.P. continues to grapple with intense heat as 95 mandals record above 42°C
content: Kanumolu in Krishna district records 45.8°C; severe heatwave conditions may prevail in 21 mandals while heatwave conditions may affect 214 mandals on Sunday, says APSDMA

title: Marco Rubio in India highlights: PM Modi, Rubio discuss bilateral ties, West Asia crisis
content: Rubio invites PM Modi to White House; they discussed India-U.S. Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership and issues related to regional and global peace and security

title: Manipur CM assures NIA probe into abduction of Naga men
content: Kuki Inpi Manipur extends shutdown since May 13 by another 48 hours to secure release of 14 Kuki people

title: Telangana proceeds with plan to relocate polluting units outside city, issues HILTP norms
content: Minister for Industries and IT D. Sridhar Babu launched a dedicated online portal for receiving applications from eligible industrial units seeking to avail benefits under the policy

title: States to get VB-G RAM G funding based on 16th Finance Commission formula, ‘performance criteria’
content: Draft rules open for feedback till June 21; 16-member National Level Steering Committee for the new scheme must include five State representatives; rules also spell out provisions for the transition from MGNREGA

title: BMCRI working on setting up a centralised diagnostic hub to ease burden on Victoria Hospital’s Infosys central laboratory
content: A sharp rise in patient load is resulting in long delays for tests and reports at the central diagnostic laboratory built by the Infosys Foundation

title: Congress slams Centre over rising fuel prices, questions PM for not disclosing seriousness of crisis
content: Congress president accuses Centre of ‘looting the public in instalments’, says benefits were not passed down to people when global prices were low

title: Experts urge hydration, awareness to prevent heat-related illnesses
content: Prolonged exposure to high temperatures can affect the body’s natural thermoregulation mechanisms and lead to a range of health conditions, experts said at a webinar organised by The Hindu on Saturday

title: India is important to the U.S.’s Indo-Pacific policy, says Rubio, invites Modi to Washington
content: The U.S. Secretary of State is in India for a four-day visit to hold bilateral talks, and attend the Quad Foreign Ministers’ Meeting

title: The ballad of Meena, a midwife, the story behind Annakili
content: Annakili, the film that introduced maestro Ilaiyaraaja to Tamil cinema, was based on a real-life, tragic tale a midwife from Pappanaickenpatti, the native village of the film’s writer R. Selvaraj, in Madurai district

title: Centre asks Delhi Gymkhana Club to hand over premises by June 5
content: The premises, located in a highly sensitive and strategic area of Delhi, is critically required for the strengthening and securing of defence infrastructure

title: A nation that makes its own weapons writes its destiny: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said future wars would increasingly be determined by advancements in munitions, automation, and emerging technologies rather than merely the size of armed forces

title: Next cabinet meeting to take final call on rolling out free-travel scheme for women on KSRTC buses
content: Transport Minister C.P. John says the details regarding the implementation of the scheme will be revealed after discussing it in the Cabinet

title: War of words between DMK and Congress over pre- and post-poll allegiances
content: A resolution adopted at the DMK youth wing meeting accused the Congress of backstabbing it after winning Assembly and Parliament seats with the efforts of DMK workers

title: Rift in Malayalam actor’s association AMMA turns murkier as actor Ansiba alleges character assassination by actor Tiny Tom
content: Ansiba cites alleged harassment as reason behind her resignation from the organisation on February 21

title: 'Vidyarthi Mitra’ kit distribution will be delayed by a month, says Samagra Shiksha official
content: Suppliers have sought more time citing shortage of gas, crude oil and raw materials such as fibre and disruptions in transportation systems due to international war-like situation, says State Project Director Srinivasa Rao

title: One more Trinamool councillor from Bidhannagar arrested
content: Charges against the arrested TMC leaders include extortion, intimidation, and money laundering; the TMC’s top leadership remains largely silent on the arrests

title: A second chance at life: Sangeetha’s brush with a rampaging elephant
content: She was trapped in her car which was right in the way of the marauding elephant. The animal lifted the car, kept it suspended in the air for a moment, dropped it back and moved on

title: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham and UTAS sign MoU
content: Pact to strengthen collaboration in research, innovation, academic exchange, and technology development

title: Two arrested for sexual assault-cum-murder of minor girl in Sulur near Coimbatore
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay expressed shock over the incident and said he has instructed the police to conduct a swift and thorough investigation

title: ‘Kumbh a great way to make infrastructure climate friendly’
content: At a session titled ‘Kumbh: The spiritual boost for a regional economy’ at The Hindu Maharashtra Infrastructure Conclave held on Friday in Mumbai, senior officials Shekhar Singh, Commissioner of Nashik Kumbh Mela, and Manisha Khatri, Commissioner of Nashik Municipal Corporation, spoke about the highlights and the challenges of the ₹35,000 crore project

title: Advocate killed after SUV rams into him in suspected hit-and-run case in Hyderabad’s Masab Tank
content: Preliminary investigation indicate the SUV may have been waiting at a short distance away before deliberately targeting the advocate

title: Textbooks to be distributed before June 1, says Minister
content: Shamsudheen said that meetings had been held with officials to ensure that delays in printing do not disrupt textbook distribution

title: Hyderabad police aim to break language barriers with AI
content: An AI-powered multilingual complaint recording tool launched by Hyderabad police that can transcribe, translate and convert spoken complaints in 10 Indian languages into FIR-ready documents in real time

title: Mumbai Metropolitan Region to contribute ₹1.7 lakh crore to Maharashtra’s economy by 2047: Shrikant Shinde
content: Ashish Shelar, Guardian Minister for Mumbai suburbs, acknowledged that the 437 sq. km. area in the city has challenges that will not be resolved in a short period

title: ‘Development is not about factories or roads; we must connect with people’
content: Maharashtra Deputy CM Eknath Shinde highlights the importance of projects in Maoist-affected areas of the State, batting for a cluster-based approach to development that covers multiple villages; he says the government’s monthly aid scheme for women will not be halted at any cost

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Arjun Tendulkar's joy turns to frustration after Rishabh Pant spills chance - WATCH
content: Arjun Tendulkar made his IPL debut for Lucknow Super Giants against Punjab Kings, bowling his first over immediately after the Powerplay and impressing with his line and length. He also remained unbeaten on five runs as LSG posted 196/6. Josh Inglis and Ayush Badoni were key contributors with the bat for LSG.

title: Swipe, scroll, repeat: How algorithms are shaping young minds
content: Social media's pervasive influence is reshaping childhood, impacting attention spans, emotional regulation, and self-esteem among Indian youth. Experts and parents highlight concerns over digital addiction, cyberbullying, and the erosion of real-world interactions. While acknowledging its benefits, the article stresses the need for balanced, mindful usage and open communication to navigate this digital landscape.

title: Arshdeep Singh scripts unwanted IPL record in nightmare spell vs LSG
content: Arshdeep Singh endured a costly spell, conceding 52 runs in three overs, setting an unwanted PBKS record. Lucknow Super Giants posted 196, driven by Josh Inglis's aggressive 72 and Ayush Badoni's quickfire 43. Despite early setbacks, LSG's strong batting performance, including contributions from Rishabh Pant and Abdul Samad, secured a competitive total.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to California group, and this is why
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: Is United States on verge of a Maduro-like military intervention in Cuba?
content: The US under Trump is intensifying pressure on Cuba, drawing comparisons to its Venezuela policy. However, experts believe Cuba's complex political structure and disciplined security apparatus make it a far riskier and potentially costlier challenge for Washington than Venezuela.

title: Twisha Sharma case timeline: Marriage, mysterious death, FIR and courtroom battle
content: A model-turned-actor's death in Bhopal has ignited a legal storm. Her family alleges dowry harassment and abetment to suicide against her husband and his family, including a retired judge. Investigations are intensifying with an SIT formed, bail rejections, and a potential CBI probe now on the horizon.

title: Huge blow for RCB! Star player leaves IPL 2026 camp just before playoffs
content: Jacob Bethell's IPL 2026 campaign with Royal Challengers Bengaluru has been cut short due to a finger injury, raising concerns for England's upcoming Test series against New Zealand. The young batter, who impressed in the Ashes, will return home for assessment, leaving his availability for the Lord's Test uncertain. England's medical team will monitor his recovery.

title: Full breakdown of USCIS memo on change in Green Card rules for H1-B, L-1, F-1 OPT
content: USCIS has issued a new policy memo requiring most foreigners seeking a green card to apply from their home country, shifting from adjustment of status within the U.S. This stricter standard emphasizes weighing negative factors like immigration violations and inconsistent conduct, demanding stronger evidence of "unusual or outstanding" positive ties to the U.S. for those applying domestically.

title: Hantavirus panic: Are we reliving Covid fears all over again?
content: Public anxiety over hantavirus is amplified by COVID-19's lingering trauma, leading to comparisons of potential pandemics. However, experts clarify that hantavirus, primarily spread through rodent contact, lacks the efficient human-to-human transmission that fueled COVID-19's global spread, making widespread outbreaks unlikely.

title: Rs 69,000 for answer sheets: CBSE portal glitch leaves students, parents baffled
content: CBSE Class 12 students face post-result anxiety due to glitches in revaluation portals, including fluctuating fees and inaccessible services. The board has extended deadlines and cited unprecedented traffic and unauthorized interference for disruptions. Students report issues with website crashes, payment failures, and unclear scanned answer sheets, leading to widespread frustration and calls for intervention.

title: 'I'd say sorry': Why Rishabh Pant apologised in LSG's last game
content: Lucknow Super Giants captain Rishabh Pant apologized to fans for a disappointing IPL 2026 season, as his team, already out of playoff contention, faced Punjab Kings.  Pant acknowledged the tough campaign, promising a 100% effort in their final league match. Punjab Kings, needing a win to stay alive, opted to bowl first.

title: Donald Trump opposes Jaxson Dart playing against women: Report
content: During a campaign event, US President Donald Trump unexpectedly praised New York Giants quarterback Jaxson Dart’s physique. He reportedly linked it to his stance against transgender athletes in women’s sports. Trump questioned if any woman could tackle Dart. He highlighted Dart’s muscular build. The comments drew mixed reactions online, with some criticizing the focus on physical appearance over politics.

title: Kylian Mbappe’s Real Madrid future in focus amid Chelsea rumors
content: Kylian Mbappe has been linked with a surprise move to Chelsea after another frustrating season at Real Madrid despite his excellent goal record. Football expert Andy Brassell believes Mbappe’s strong connection with Xabi Alonso could play a key role in the future. Chelsea were interested in Mbappe during his youth years, and fresh transfer speculation has now returned again.

title: Toronto Maple Leafs face uncertainty amid Matthew Knies trade rumors
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs have several offseason questions to answer, and the trade talk surrounding forward Matthew Knies is heating up. Knies is still young and promising, but there is speculation that he might be used in a major roster move. Meanwhile, San Jose Sharks defenceman Mario Ferraro has emerged as a possible defensive target, adding to the complexity of Toronto’s NHL offseason plans.

title: Raveena Tandon urges Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann to keep stray dog drive humane
content: Raveena Tandon has urged Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann to handle the state's stray dog management with compassion, following a Supreme Court order. While acknowledging public safety concerns, Tandon advocated for humane solutions like sterilization and shelters, suggesting Punjab could lead by example in balancing animal welfare with citizen security.

title: Watch: Pentagon releases footage of US jet shooting down mysterious UFO
content: Pentagon has released a new video showing a US fighter jet downing an unidentified object over Lake Huron in February 2023. This infrared footage, part of a declassified batch, depicts a "kinetic interaction" leading to the object's fragmentation. The release includes hundreds of files, documents, and videos related to "unidentified anomalous phenomena" (UAP), fueling public curiosity.

title: Not brain drain but brain circulation: Why 70% of Indian graduates are heading abroad
content: Indian graduates are increasingly pursuing higher education abroad, with nearly 70% planning to study overseas for better exposure and skills. However, most intend to return to India for employment, reflecting growing confidence in the domestic job market. The trend signals a shift from traditional brain drain to “brain circulation” driven by global learning and local opportunities.

title: From PMAY to DDA: Government housing schemes every homebuyer should know
content: For countless individuals, the dream of owning a home seems increasingly out of reach due to escalating prices and limited access to financing. In response, governments are launching a spectrum of housing initiatives.

title: Centre loosens purse strings for Bengal after Suvendu Adhikari's first Delhi visit as CM
content: Following Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari's Delhi visit, the Centre has approved Rs 39,000 crore for West Bengal's water resources ministry schemes. Prime Minister Modi pledged support for central projects and economic development, aiming to boost welfare initiatives like Ayushman Bharat and rural employment. Discussions also covered border security with Home Minister Amit Shah.

title: 'Potential to diversify': Rubio pitches stronger US-India energy ties during meeting with PM Modi
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio affirmed Washington's commitment to preventing geopolitical disruptions from impacting global energy markets, particularly amid tensions linked to the Iran conflict. He highlighted US energy exports as a tool to strengthen partnerships, especially with India, aiming to diversify its supply and reduce reliance on other sources.

title: The big AI shock: Have Indian IT sector stocks lost their lustre?
content: In fact, ⁠IT has been the worst-performing major sector in the Indian market over the past 12-18 months. In May, 2026, the Nifty IT index touched a near 3-year low, losing more than a fourth of its value since the start of the year.

title: Do not invite: Why Donald Trump offboarded Tulsi Gabbard
content: The gulf between Trump’s actions and Gabbard’s worldview made the latter’s position untenable. Perhaps her fate was sealed last June when, less than two weeks before Operation Midnight Hammer, she posted a video in which she said “political elite warmongers are carelessly fomenting fear and tensions between nuclear powers”. The post appeared shortly before the US struck Iran’s nuclear sites in an operation almost borrowed from the plot of Top Gun: Maverick.

title: Twisha Sharma's family lawyer claims Samarth Singh 'hid' inside courtroom before arrest
content: Twisha Sharma's family lawyer alleges her husband, Samarth Singh, was 'hidden' in a locked courtroom before his arrest in Jabalpur. Singh was taken into custody by police after arriving to surrender, with a lookout notice and reward previously issued for his arrest. The case intensifies as the High Court allows a second postmortem and the state recommends a CBI probe.

title: Selling a property? Here’s all you need to know about capital gains tax rules
content: Capital gains tax rules on Property changed from July 23, 2024. Long-term gains now face a 12.5% tax without indexation, a shift from the previous 20% with indexation.  However, those who bought property before this date can choose the old or new system.  Exemptions under Sections 54, 54F, and 54EC remain available.

title: Why Jasprit Bumrah struggled in IPL? Jayawardene reveals shocking reason
content: Mumbai Indians coach Mahela Jayawardene revealed Jasprit Bumrah battled a niggle from the T20 World Cup into IPL 2026, impacting his performance.  Despite a slow start, Bumrah regained pace and sharpness in later games, though the team's campaign ended early.  Jayawardene emphasized Bumrah's strong work ethic and recovery.

title: ‘To really be accepting of myself was my hardest lesson...': Quote of the day by Julia Roberts
content: In a heartfelt moment during her latest talk, Julia Roberts reflects on a hard-earned lesson: the necessity of self-acceptance. The beloved star underlines that true contentment stems from taking pride in our authentic selves, insecurities and all. Embracing our complexities, rather than conforming to others' ideals, she argues, is foundational for mental well-being and personal growth.

title: Dana White compares Conor McGregor to Muhammad Ali ahead of UFC return
content: Dana White has never been afraid to make bold statements. The UFC president once again grabbed attention after comparing two of MMA’s biggest stars to legends from other sports. White said Conor McGregor is similar to Muhammad Ali because of his worldwide fame and ability to attract fans. He also described Jon Jones as the Michael Jordan of MMA because of his dominance inside the cage.

title: 'Nobody can outdo us in violence': Bangladesh’s student-led NCP issues warning to ruling BNP
content: Bangladesh's National Citizen Party spokesperson Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain issued a stern warning to the ruling party, asserting their readiness for violence if provoked. Following an attack on a party leader, Bhuyain demanded arrests, threatening a generational battle if the government persists with aggression.

title: Found your dream home? Here’s how to check if it fits your budget
content: Buying a home requires careful financial planning beyond just the property price. EMI calculator tools, like those provided by SBI and HDFC, are crucial for assessing affordability. They consider your income, existing loans, and desired loan terms to estimate monthly payments and borrowing capacity, ensuring a smart financial decision and preventing overborrowing.

title: 'Neither unemployed nor lazy but ... ': Sonam Wangchuk calls himself 'an honorary cockroach'
content: Sonam Wangchuk, calling himself an "honorary cockroach," lauded the satirical "Cockroach Janata Party" movement, urging the government to heed youth concerns instead of suppressing digital dissent. He likened the campaign's creative, non-violent approach to political cartoons, advocating for engagement over censorship to prevent frustration from escalating.

title: Vinesh Phogat cleared for Asian Games trials as Delhi HC calls WFI policy unfair
content: Delhi High Court has permitted star wrestler Vinesh Phogat to compete in Asian Games trials, criticizing the Wrestling Federation of India's rigid policy that penalizes athletes returning from maternity leave. The court emphasized fairness, allowing Phogat's participation and ordering transparent, supervised selection proceedings to uphold the sport's integrity and justice.

title: Centre orders Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate premises by June 5
content: The Delhi Gymkhana Club must vacate its 27.3-acre Lutyens' Delhi land by June 5. The government requires the prime location for defence infrastructure and security. The Land and Development Office issued the order, determining the lease. Possession will be taken on June 5. Non-compliance will result in legal action. The club, established in 1913, is in a strategic zone.

title: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi plays only as Impact Player for RR
content: Rajasthan's 15-year-old batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, an Impact Player, faces scrutiny from former India players for not fielding. Assistant coach Trevor Penny defends the decision, citing Sooryavanshi's fielding skills and the strategy to keep him fresh for his explosive batting, which has impressed many.

title: Future wars will span cyber, cognitive domains: CDS Anil Chauhan
content: Future conflicts will span multiple domains including land, sea, air, cyberspace, and cognitive warfare, Chief of Defence Staff Anil Chauhan stated at the inauguration of a defence manufacturing complex in Shirdi. He emphasized that technological advancements like AI and drones are reshaping battlefields, which will extend beyond geographical limits into digital realms.

title: Ivan Toney reacts to shock England World Cup call-up in viral clip
content: England striker Ivan Toney could not hide his excitement after receiving a surprise call-up to England’s FIFA World Cup squad. The Al-Ahli forward was visiting his favourite barber shop in Clapham, South London, shortly after learning the news. His reaction was captured on video and later shared online by HD CUTZ. Toney appeared relaxed and happy as he sat in the barber’s chair while speaking about representing England at the biggest tournament in football.

title: Ravindra reveals how Kohli and Rahane shaped his Test preparation
content: Rachin Ravindra returns home from IPL early. He feels ready for New Zealand's Test matches in Ireland and England.  Ravindra gained valuable insights from top players like Ajinkya Rahane and Virat Kohli.  He believes these conversations and training sessions have prepared him for challenging conditions.  The experience has boosted his confidence for the upcoming red-ball assignments.

title: Trump To Award Highest Civilian Honour To 9/11 Hero Who Died Saving 18 People
content: On the morning of the attacks, Welles Remy Crowther had called his mother to tell her he was safe after the planes struck. Hours later, his body was recovered from the wreckage of the collapsed South Tower.

title: Supreme Court Takes Up Twisha Sharma Case, Hearing On May 25
content: Supreme Court takes up Twisha Sharma case, Chief Justice of India Surya Kant to hear matter on May 25

title: 'Dragged, Pulled By Hair': Flotilla Activists Recount Torture By Israel
content: Israel denies mistreatment. The allegations were "false and entirely without factual basis," said Zivan Freidin, a spokesperson for the Israeli Prison Service.

title: Efforts To End Iran War On, No Timeline On Strikes: Marco Rubio To NDTV
content: Rubio told NDTV that US President Donald Trump prefers to solve these two problems - the nuclear issue and opening the Strait of Hormuz through a negotiated agreement and diplomacy.

title: Raghav Chadha Appointed Chairman Of Rajya Sabha Committee On Petitions
content: Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha who switched from the AAP to the BJP recently has been appointed as the new chairman of the Committee on Petitions of the Upper House.

title: US Announces "America First" Visa Tool For "Vital" India Ties. What It Means
content: The tool is built around a single prioritisation principle: applicants who strengthen ties with the United States get earlier access to scheduling.

title: After PM's 'Satisfactory' Appraisal Rating, Ministers Swing Into Action
content: The chief yardstick for the performance appraisal was the ministries' agility in complaint redressal and file disposal.

title: Delhi Continues To Sizzles As Mercury Crosses 44-Degrees Celcius Mark
content: Dry scorching winds and relentless heat turned Delhi into a furnace on Monday as the maximum temperatures breached the 44 degrees Celsius mark in parts of the national capital, with the city staring at an extended spell of punishing heat in the upcoming week.

title: 'Samarth Singh Showed No Emotion After Wife's Death': Twisha Sharma's Cousin
content: The cousin of alleged dowry death victim Twisha Sharma, on Saturday, claimed that her husband Samarth Singh who had "surrendered" a day before, did not display any emotions during court hearings in the case.

title: Special AIIMS Delhi Board To Conduct Twisha Sharma's 2nd Autopsy In Bhopal
content: The court observed that since the death took place within six months of marriage, a second post-mortem was necessary in the interest of transparency and public confidence.

title: India To Become Major Arms Exporter In 25-30 Years: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Saturday said no power can stop India, once considered an importer of weapons, from becoming the biggest exporter in 25-30 years.

title: "It's Not About India": Marco Rubio To NDTV On New US Greencard Rule
content: "Well, it's not about India. It's about the whole world," US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told NDTV today on the new green card policy

title: RG Kar Case: Bengal Starts Departmental Proceedings Against Suspended Doctor
content: The West Bengal government on Saturday initiated departmental proceedings against a doctor suspended following the rape and murder at RG Kar Medical College and Hospital, and also ordered a separate enquiry into his admission to a postgraduate course

title: 3 Girls Drown In Stepwell While Bathing In Madhya Pradesh
content: Three minor girls, including two sisters, drowned while bathing in a stepwell in Madhya Pradesh's Raisen district on Saturday, police said.

title: Explained: Why Buyers Are Suddenly Ditching 1BHK, 2BHK Flats in Noida
content: The real estate market in Gautam Buddha Nagar is witnessing a dramatic transformation, with buyers increasingly choosing spacious luxury apartments over compact homes.

title: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: "Iran War Will Be Solved One Way Or The Other": Marco Rubio To NDTV
content: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: The top US diplomat is scheduled to hold talks with External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and attend a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers.

title: 'No More Slums In Mumbai': Eknath Shinde Unveils Massive Cluster Redevelopment Push
content: Shinde said the government has already formed 19 cluster redevelopment zones across the Mumbai Metropolitan Region.

title: Marco Rubio Meets PM Modi, Highlights India-US Progress Across Sectors
content: Security has been stepped up at the 'jhalmuri' stall, which Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited during his campaign for the West Bengal election last month, after its owner allegedly received death threats, police said on Saturday.

title: From H1B to 'maybe': Green card dream hits red light
content: a sweeping new directive issued by the u.s. Citizenship and Immigration Services . most temporary visa holders seeking permanent residency will now be required to leave the U.S. and apply for green cards from their home countries . the policy marks one of the most dramatic attempts by the Trump administration to curb illegal immigration .

title: 'They share personal relation': Rubio invites PM Modi on behalf of Trump to visit White House
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
